chunks.title: Catalog Description / Additional Course Details / Special Course Designation
chunks.content:
Computer Organization. (3-2). Credit 4. Computer systems from programmer's perspective including simple logic design, data representation and processor architecture, programming of processors, memory, control flow, input/output, and performance measurements; hands-on lab assignments. Prerequisite: Grade of C or better in CSCE 221, or concurrent enrollment.

Topics covered in the course are:
• Computer System
• Binary Representation
• Boolean Logic Design
• Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU)
• Sequential Design
• Machine Language
• CPU Architecture (Processor)
• Memory
• Virtual Memory
• Assembler
• Advanced Topics (based on time availability)

None

chunks.title: Course Prerequisites
chunks.content: Prerequisite/Corequisite(s): Grade of C or better in CSCE 221, or concurrent enrollment.

chunks.title: Course Learning Outcomes
chunks.content:
Upon completion of the course, students will be able to:
• Perform Basic Logic Design
• Understand the design of a simple computer system
• Understand elements that make up a CPU (Central Processing Unit)
• Design software with an understanding of how it will run on computers
• Participate in discussions that involve impact of hardware and software elements on each other and the performance of the program
• Take intermediate and advanced classes in computer architecture, systems, operating systems, cloud, parallel computing

chunks.content:

chunks.title: Late Work Policy / Course Specific Late Work Policy / Course Specific Attendance Policy / Course Specific Makeup Work Policy
chunks.content:
Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy (Student Rule 7).

No late submissions for assignments (i.e. lab exercises) will be allowed without an excused medical or family event reason. To receive approval for a medical or serious family event, you must send email to the TA, with necessary documentation, such as a Dr's note or event note, no later than 3 days from assignment due date. You must receive approval form the TA. In case you do not receive a response from the TA within 2 business days, send me a reminder and cc the Instructor. An approval must be in place for full credit relating to the missed assignment. For circumstances other than the above, contact the instructor for approval.
For late submissions without excused extension the following reduction will be applied:
• 80% of score received on assignment for late submission no greater than 3 days from deadline
• 60% of score received on assignment for late submission more than 3 days but no greater than 1 week from deadline
• 40% of score received on assignment for late submission more than 1 week but no greater than 2 weeks from deadline
• 0% otherwise

Attendance is mandatory for all lectures and laboratories except for the following situations:
• For medical reasons, which requires documentation and an email to the TA (for Lab) or to the Instructor (for Lectures)
• For immediate family health reasons. Also requires documentation and email to TA or Instructor.
• 2 unexcused absences are allowed for lectures and for labs each (ie a total of 4).

If you miss a mid semester exam, you will have to schedule a separate exam with the TA within 1 week of the missed exam. Note that only documented medical or family situations can be reasons for reschedule.
If you miss the final exam, contact the instructor immediately.
If you have 3 exams on May 1st (finals) contact the instructor for alternate times no later than 10 days prior to the final exam.

chunks.title: AI Statement
chunks.content:
• You are not allowed to use any AI tool for any assignment, lab work, exams or reports.
• All detected plagiarism, cheating, and complicity will be reported to the Aggie Honor System Office.
• The sanction is a zero on the assignment and a letter grade reduction for a single infraction.
• The sanction can be a letter grade For F* for a pattern of infractions.
• Academic dishonesty will not be tolerated. For assignments, such as status reports or poster creation, the group is expected to write their material. Do not use AI tools! Not even for polishing your report or correcting grammar. Use spellcheckers and grammar checkers in document editors.
• Students should not have another person/entity do the writing of any portion of any assignment for them, which includes hiring a person or a company to write assignments and using artificial intelligence tools like ChatGPT, Gemini.
• Every student should understand that complicity – helping or attempting to help another student commit an act of academic dishonesty – also constitutes academic dishonesty and carries the same punishment as cheating and plagiarism.
• Example if you have direct knowledge that another person on the class is using AI tools for unapproved work, and you do not report it immediately, you will be subject to the same sanction as the individual committing plagiarism or unauthorized access of AI tools.
